restaurants.title: Vato
restaurants.review:
What's not to like. Fire tortillas, great baked goods. The only shitty thing is the line but what are you gonna do it's great food. Also their egg burritos aren't for me, but I'm just not an egg guy.

I'd honestly argue you should just get the bean and cheese burrito because the tortillas are the star of the show.

restaurants.title: SottoVoce
restaurants.review: Super family style vibe, the people there really care more about the food they serve than appearing any sort of way (complimentary). The sauces we got had clear depth and thought put into what the experience of eating the pasta would be like. The vodka sauce I had was creamy and smokey, but very subtle without completely losing any complexity. I was really impressed, I'm a huge Italian hater, so good job SottoVoce.

restaurants.title: 7th Ave Donuts
restaurants.review: It's a friendly local diner, cash only nothing crazy.

restaurants.title: WINNER
restaurants.review: meeeh I haven't had their bread but I've only had one good sandwich there. The place is overpriced, but their chicken salad sandwich is really good. I'd honestly skip it there are better places to get food in the neighborhood.

restaurants.title: Little Purity
restaurants.review:
The food here is fine, they make their own sauces and such in house but it's nothing that'll blow your mind.

Little Purity gets 5 stars because it is one of the friendliest, most welcoming places you'll go to in the city. They've been there for a couple decades, and the brother and sister duo who run it are really in it for the love of the game. I encourage whoever is reading this, to take some time, get a coffee, and meet the fun and kind owners of this place.

restaurants.title: Norm's Pizza
restaurants.review: Eh. Probably some of the worst pizza I've had in NY, the place barely has a menu, and it closes early so it really has nothing going for it. There's a Joe's around the corner and a Smiling Pizza up north a couple blocks that at least make a decent slice.

restaurants.title: Crosta
restaurants.review: Good pizza, nothing wild, there's a lot of competition in this neighborhood so it's tough but it's worth giving a try if you're near by. I wouldn't go out of my way for it.

restaurants.title: Brookvin
restaurants.review: I'm not a wine guy but I liked what I had here, the vibe was really nice but this place really shines with the excellent staff. Everyone there is so friendly, they'll remember your name, it's a great place to go to as a local and get to know the staff.

restaurants.title: Locanda Mariella
restaurants.review: Really home-y Italian joint, lots of competition in this neighborhood, I think it's a nice vibe, their specials are always interesting, but it's not the best Italian I've ever had.

droppedRestaurants.title: American Cheese
droppedRestaurants.review: Awesome dive-y bar with good pizza. Most "pizza bars" have passable slices but American Cheez put a bit more effort/thought into it. The bartenders are super friendly, nothing glamorous but a great place to land during a night out.
